title: What to Ask on a Memory Care Tour
summary: Memory care tours deserve a slightly different question set than a standard assisted living visit — here's what to focus on.
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: What to Ask on a Memory Care Tour | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Memory care tours deserve a slightly different question set than a standard assisted living visit — here's what to focus on.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Ask specifically how the community handles agitation or behavioral changes.
Activity programming should be dementia-appropriate, not just generally "senior" activities.
Family communication practices matter enormously, especially as a resident's ability to report their own day declines.

paragraphs: It's worth asking directly how staff typically respond to agitation, resistance to care, or wandering attempts — the answer reveals a lot about whether the approach is patient and skilled or more about managing behavior.

Since a resident with dementia often can't reliably report back on their own day, ask specifically how the community communicates with families — regular check-ins, care plan updates, and how incidents (falls, behavioral changes) are reported.

nextSteps: Ask specifically how agitation and behavioral changes are handled.
Review the daily activity calendar for dementia-appropriate programming.
Clarify exactly how and how often the community communicates with families.
disclaimer: General guidance — touring multiple communities and trusting your direct observations matters as much as any single question.
